perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the provisions of this Agreement, the obligations and restrictions set forth herein regarding Confidential Information shall not apply to information that the Receiving Party can establish (i) is or becomes publicly available other than as a result of a breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party, (ii) is lawfully received from a third party which is, to the reasonable knowledge and belief of the Receiving Party, not under any obligation of confidentiality for the benefit of the Disclosing Party, (iii) was either in the possession of or known to the Receiving Party at the time of disclosure without any limitation on use or disclosure for the benefit of the Receiving Party, or (iv) is independently developed by the Receiving Party without the use, r eference to or benefit of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the provisions of this Agreement, the obligations and restrictions set forth herein regarding Confidential Information shall not apply to information that the Receiving Party can establish (i) is or becomes publicly available other than as a result of a breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party, (ii) is lawfully received from a third party which is, to the reasonable knowledge and belief of the Receiving Party, not under any obligation of confidentiality for the benefit of the Disclosing Party, (iii) was either in the possession of or known to the Receiving Party at the time of disclosure without any limitation on use or disclosure for the benefit of the Receiving Party, or (iv) is independently developed by the Receiving Party without the use, reference to or benefit of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information. **This exception does not apply if the information concerns potential funders, joint venture partners, banks, etc. introduced to Party 1 by Party 2.**
perturbation.explanation: The original text provides exceptions to the confidentiality obligations. The added sentence creates a contradiction by stating that the exceptions *do not* apply to specific business contacts introduced by Party 2 to Party 1. This means the confidentiality obligation is absolute for those contacts, even if the information later becomes publicly available or was known to the Receiving Party before. The rest of Section 3 details broader exceptions, creating internal inconsistency.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding the provisions of this Agreement, the obligations and restrictions set forth herein regarding Confidential Information shall not apply to information that the Receiving Party can establish (i) is or becomes publicly available other than as a result of a breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party, (ii) is lawfully received from a third party which is, to the reasonable knowledge and belief of the Receiving Party, not under any obligation of confidentiality for the benefit of the Disclosing Party, (iii) was either in the possession of or known to the Receiving Party at the time of disclosure without any limitation on use or disclosure for the benefit of the Receiving Party, or (iv) is independently developed by the Receiving Party without the use, reference to or benefit of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information. **This exception does not apply if the information concerns potential funders, joint venture partners, banks, etc. introduced to Party 1 by Party 2.**
perturbation.justification: The added sentence in the changed text, 'This exception does not apply if the information concerns potential funders, joint venture partners, banks, etc. introduced to Party 1 by Party 2,' creates a direct contradiction. The original text, and the first part of the changed text, states broad exceptions to confidentiality. However, the added sentence carves out a specific set of circumstances where those exceptions no longer apply, even if the information falls under one of the listed exceptions like becoming publicly available. This directly conflicts with the broader exceptions initially stated, creating a strong in-text contradiction because the contradiction is clear and direct.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the earlier of (i) the completion or (ii) the Disclosing Party’s written r equest, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all Confidential Information disclosed or made available by the Disclosing Party, in any form and including, all Notes, save to the extent that regulation, applicable rule, law or record retention policy requires retention of such material. Upon request, the Receiving Party shall certify in writing that it has returned or destroyed all Confidential Information in accordance with this paragraph 6, disclosed or made available by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the earlier of (i) the completion or (ii) the Disclosing Party’s written r equest, the Receiving Party may, at their discretion, return or destroy all Confidential Information disclosed or made available by the Disclosing Party, in any form and including, all Notes, save to the extent that regulation, applicable rule, law or record retention policy requires retention of such material. Upon request, the Receiving Party shall certify in writing that it has returned or destroyed all Confidential Information in accordance with this paragraph 6, disclosed or made available by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the Receiving Party to return or destroy all Confidential Information upon request or completion. The altered text changes 'shall promptly return or destroy' to 'may, at their discretion, return or destroy.' This introduces a contradiction because, under the original phrasing, the Receiving Party *must* destroy the information, while the altered phrasing gives them the *option* not to. This contradicts other sections that assume mandatory destruction of confidential information, creating uncertainty about data security and compliance with non-disclosure obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties anticipate that in connection with, any of the Parties may receive or become privy to the Confidential Infor mation of the other Parties. A Receiving Party agrees, acknowledges and undertakes to (i) hold the ConfidentialInformation of the Disclosing Party in strictest confidence, and subject to the terms ofthis Agreement, not to disclose Confidential Information to any third party unless it is aclose family member of the receiving party .Such information shall be shared freely. Where the Party does not know the intended thirdparty then the Party shall ask for prior written consent of the Disclosing Party (which maybe given on such terms as the Disclosing Party considers appropriate), (ii) not to use theConfidential Information for any purpose (including but not limited to any competitive or Tabun Kitchen 151 Copse Hill, London, SW20 0SU2commercial purpose) other than in relation to the PR activities as contemplated under any other written agreement between the parties which references this Agreement, and (iii) protect such Confidential Information by using the same degree of care as it uses to protect its own highly Confidential Information of similar value and sensitivity, but not less than reasonable care.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies a mandatory action ('shall promptly return or destroy') to a discretionary action ('may, at their discretion, return or destroy'). The contradicted text states that the Receiving Party 'agrees, acknowledges and undertakes to (i) hold the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party in strictest confidence'. The modification creates a contradiction because the Receiving Party no longer has a mandatory obligation to return or destroy the Confidential Information, potentially conflicting with the agreement to hold the information in strictest confidence.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations undertaken by each party under this Agreement will be continuing and in particular shall survive termination of any discussions or negotiations between the parties regarding the funding activities following written notice from one party to the other party expressly terminating this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations undertaken by each party under this Agreement will be continuing and in particular shall survive termination of any discussions or negotiations between the parties regarding the funding activities following written notice from one party to the other party expressly terminating this Agreement. **However, this survival shall not exceed one year from the termination date.**
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the confidentiality obligations are continuing and survive termination of the agreement. The added sentence introduces a time limit of one year. This creates a contradiction: previously, the obligations were indefinite, but now they expire after one year. The effect is that confidential information could be used or disclosed freely after this period, which may conflict with the overall intent to protect sensitive data indefinitely.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations undertaken by each party under this Agreement will be continuing and in particular shall survive termination of any discussions or negotiations between the parties regarding the funding activities following written notice from one party to the other party expressly terminating this Agreement. **However, this survival shall not exceed one year from the termination date.**
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the obligations survive termination indefinitely. The changed text adds a one-year limit to this survival. This directly contradicts the original intent of perpetual survival, creating a clear inconsistency. The contradicted text, being identical to the changed text, reinforces this contradiction because the indefinite obligation is now explicitly limited to one year, causing a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Receiving Party acknowledges that a violation of this Agreement by the Receiving Party may cause irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party and that the Disclosing Party may have no adequate remedy at law. Accordingly, each party agrees that the Disclosing Party shall have the right, in addition to any other rights and Tabun Kitchen 151 Copse Hill, London, SW20 0SU3 remedies it may have, at law, in equity or otherwise, to seek injunctive relief or similar such orders in any court of competent jurisdiction directing or prohibiting certain acts which may be necessary to protect the Confidential Information and the Disclosing Party. This Clause 9 shall not apply for the mere evaluation of non-patented, non-copyrighted information such as Business Plans, Business Proposals etc that have no quantifiable valuation nor unique intellectual value. This Clause 9 shall apply to valuable contacts intr oduced by either Party with regards to investors, bankers, financiers and other professionals.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Receiving Party acknowledges that a violation of this Agreement by the Receiving Party may cause irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party and that the Disclosing Party may have no adequate remedy at law. Accordingly, each party agrees that the Disclosing Party shall have the right, in addition to any other rights and Tabun Kitchen 151 Copse Hill, London, SW20 0SU3 remedies it may have, at law, in equity or otherwise, to seek injunctive relief or similar such orders in any court of competent jurisdiction directing or prohibiting certain acts which may be necessary to protect the Confidential Information and the Disclosing Party. This Clause 9 shall not apply for the mere evaluation of non-patented, non-copyrighted information such as Business Plans, Business Proposals etc that have no quantifiable valuation nor unique intellectual value. **This Clause 9 shall not apply if the Receiving Party is a close family member of the Receiving Party.** This Clause 9 shall apply to valuable contacts intr oduced by either Party with regards to investors, bankers, financiers and other professionals.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Disclosing party may seek injunctive relief for breaches to the agreement. The added sentence creates a contradiction by stating that this clause shall not apply if the Receiving Party is a close family member of the Receiving Party. This directly contradicts the entire agreement by stating the clause does not apply if the Receiving party is a close family member. This makes the agreement enforecement difficult.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Receiving Party acknowledges that a violation of this Agreement by the Receiving Party may cause irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party and that the Disclosing Party may have no adequate remedy at law. Accordingly, each party agrees that the Disclosing Party shall have the right, in addition to any other rights and Tabun Kitchen 151 Copse Hill, London, SW20 0SU3 remedies it may have, at law, in equity or otherwise, to seek injunctive relief or similar such orders in any court of competent jurisdiction directing or prohibiting certain acts which may be necessary to protect the Confidential Information and the Disclosing Party. This Clause 9 shall not apply for the mere evaluation of non-patented, non-copyrighted information such as Business Plans, Business Proposals etc that have no quantifiable valuation nor unique intellectual value. **This Clause 9 shall not apply if the Receiving Party is a close family member of the Receiving Party.** This Clause 9 shall apply to valuable contacts intr oduced by either Party with regards to investors, bankers, financiers and other professionals.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text states that Clause 9, regarding injunctive relief for breaches, applies to all Receiving Parties. The changed text adds an exception: Clause 9 does not apply if the Receiving Party is a close family member of the Disclosing Party. This directly contradicts the original intent of the agreement to protect Confidential Information through injunctive relief, creating an inconsistency and making the agreement's enforcement unclear, as stated in the Perturbation Explanation.
